title: althaea vulgaris
intro: Althaea vulgaris, also known as marshmallow plant, is a perennial herb native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, characterized by its large, soft, and velvety leaves. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and soothing effects, making it an effective treatment for various health issues. The health benefits of Althaea vulgaris include soothing digestive problems, calming skin irritations, and alleviating coughs and respiratory issues. Althaea vulgaris is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or syrup, and its root is also used to make a powder or extract, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
